verses.text: The priest examines a rising or spot; white hair and depth mark leprosy. Examined. The priest discerns the plague.
The priest examines a rising or spot; white hair and depth mark leprosy. Examined. The priest discerns the plague.
The priest examines a rising or spot; white hair and depth mark leprosy. Examined. The priest discerns the plague.
If it spreads not, he is shut up and seen again. Shut up. Time and watching test the plague.
If it spreads not, he is shut up and seen again. Shut up. Time and watching test the plague.
If it spreads not, he is shut up and seen again. Shut up. Time and watching test the plague.
If it spreads, he is pronounced unclean. Unclean. The spreading plague defiles.
If it spreads, he is pronounced unclean. Unclean. The spreading plague defiles.
An old leprosy in the skin is at once unclean. Old plague. Settled leprosy is plain.
An old leprosy in the skin is at once unclean. Old plague. Settled leprosy is plain.
An old leprosy in the skin is at once unclean. Old plague. Settled leprosy is plain.
If it covers all the flesh and turns white, he is clean. All white. Full covering is judged clean.
If it covers all the flesh and turns white, he is clean. All white. Full covering is judged clean.
But raw flesh appearing makes him unclean. Raw flesh. The living sore defiles.
But raw flesh appearing makes him unclean. Raw flesh. The living sore defiles.
But raw flesh appearing makes him unclean. Raw flesh. The living sore defiles.
But raw flesh appearing makes him unclean. Raw flesh. The living sore defiles.
A boil that turns to a white rising is judged unclean. The boil. An old sore may breed the plague.
A boil that turns to a white rising is judged unclean. The boil. An old sore may breed the plague.
A boil that turns to a white rising is judged unclean. The boil. An old sore may breed the plague.
If the spot stays and spreads not, he is clean. Watched. Not every mark is leprosy.
If the spot stays and spreads not, he is clean. Watched. Not every mark is leprosy.
If the spot stays and spreads not, he is clean. Watched. Not every mark is leprosy.
A burning that turns to a spot is likewise examined and judged. The burning. Each case is carefully weighed.
A burning that turns to a spot is likewise examined and judged. The burning. Each case is carefully weighed.
A burning that turns to a spot is likewise examined and judged. The burning. Each case is carefully weighed.
A burning that turns to a spot is likewise examined and judged. The burning. Each case is carefully weighed.
A burning that turns to a spot is likewise examined and judged. The burning. Each case is carefully weighed.
A plague on head or beard is examined for the scall. The scall. The priest discerns hidden plagues.
A plague on head or beard is examined for the scall. The scall. The priest discerns hidden plagues.
A plague on head or beard is examined for the scall. The scall. The priest discerns hidden plagues.
A plague on head or beard is examined for the scall. The scall. The priest discerns hidden plagues.
He is shut up and shaved; if the scall stays, he is clean. Cleared. Patience proves the case.
He is shut up and shaved; if the scall stays, he is clean. Cleared. Patience proves the case.
He is shut up and shaved; if the scall stays, he is clean. Cleared. Patience proves the case.
He is shut up and shaved; if the scall stays, he is clean. Cleared. Patience proves the case.
He is shut up and shaved; if the scall stays, he is clean. Cleared. Patience proves the case.
Bright darkish spots are but a freckled spot, clean. Freckles. Not every blemish is leprosy.
Bright darkish spots are but a freckled spot, clean. Freckles. Not every blemish is leprosy.
Baldness with a reddish-white sore may be leprosy. Baldness. The plague may rise even there.
Baldness with a reddish-white sore may be leprosy. Baldness. The plague may rise even there.
Baldness with a reddish-white sore may be leprosy. Baldness. The plague may rise even there.
Baldness with a reddish-white sore may be leprosy. Baldness. The plague may rise even there.
Baldness with a reddish-white sore may be leprosy. Baldness. The plague may rise even there.
The leper cries 'Unclean,' dwelling alone outside the camp. Outside. The defiled is shut out from the people.
The leper cries 'Unclean,' dwelling alone outside the camp. Outside. The defiled is shut out from the people.
A plague in a garment is shown to the priest. In a garment. Even cloth can carry the plague.
A plague in a garment is shown to the priest. In a garment. Even cloth can carry the plague.
A plague in a garment is shown to the priest. In a garment. Even cloth can carry the plague.
If it spreads after shutting up, the garment is burnt. Burnt. The infected thing is destroyed.
If it spreads after shutting up, the garment is burnt. Burnt. The infected thing is destroyed.
If it spreads after shutting up, the garment is burnt. Burnt. The infected thing is destroyed.
If it spreads not but fades not, it is burnt. Tested. What stays unclean is destroyed.
If it spreads not but fades not, it is burnt. Tested. What stays unclean is destroyed.
If it spreads not but fades not, it is burnt. Tested. What stays unclean is destroyed.
If it fades after washing, the garment is clean. Cleansed. What is cleansed may be kept.
If it fades after washing, the garment is clean. Cleansed. What is cleansed may be kept.
If it fades after washing, the garment is clean. Cleansed. What is cleansed may be kept.
If it fades after washing, the garment is clean. Cleansed. What is cleansed may be kept.
